description: Redesign the /app/endpoint page to show 3 tunnel management cards (Cloudflared, Tailscale, Ngrok) with status badges, public URLs, start/stop buttons, and an Available Endpoints section
steps: Step 1: Create src/app/(dashboard)/app/endpoint/components/TunnelCard.tsx - a reusable shadcn/ui Card component that displays tunnel status (running/stopped/error), public URL with copy button, start/stop action button, and last error message. Props: tunnelName, status object (phase, publicUrl, apiUrl, running, lastError), onStart callback, onStop callback, isLoading state.

Step 2: Create src/app/(dashboard)/app/endpoint/components/TunnelsSection.tsx - renders 3 TunnelCards (Cloudflared, Tailscale, Ngrok). Uses TanStack React Query to fetch from /api/tunnels/status with polling every 5s. Implements start/stop mutations for each tunnel calling the respective /api/tunnels/<tunnel>/start and /api/tunnels/<tunnel>/stop endpoints. For Ngrok, include an auth token input field if status is 'needs_auth'. For Tailscale, show auth URL link when in needs_login phase.

Step 3: Create src/app/(dashboard)/app/endpoint/components/AvailableEndpoints.tsx - shows a list of active provider endpoints. Fetches from /api/providers to get the provider list. For each active/connected provider, displays: provider name, icon, the local endpoint URL (http://localhost:12711/v1), and any active tunnel URLs (pulled from tunnel status). Each URL row has a copy-to-clipboard button using navigator.clipboard.writeText(). Styled with shadcn/ui Table or Card list pattern.

Step 4: Update src/app/(dashboard)/app/endpoint/EndpointPageClient.tsx to add a 'Tunnels' tab alongside 'Main' and 'Protocols'. The Tunnels tab renders TunnelsSection. Also add AvailableEndpoints below the main content area (visible in all tabs or as a separate section).
Step 5: Ensure all new components use shadcn/ui (Card, Button, Badge, Input, Table) and Tailwind CSS only. No OmniRoute CSS variables.
Step 6: Run npm run typecheck and npm run build to validate.
acceptance_criteria: TunnelCard component exists and renders tunnel status with start/stop buttons
TunnelsSection fetches from /api/tunnels/status and renders 3 tunnel cards
AvailableEndpoints component shows active provider endpoints with copy buttons
All styling uses shadcn/ui + Tailwind only
findings: Build debug messages about node:fs in edge runtime are pre-existing and unrelated to our changes. Badge component exists and works well for status display. The providers API returns an array of connection objects with id, provider, name fields. Used plain fetch instead of fetchJson for tunnel endpoints to keep things consistent with simple error handling. AvailableEndpoints is rendered below all tabs as a persistent section.
